words.def: a man who works in business, especially at a high level
words.example: a group of visiting Italian politicians and businessmen

words.def: a small round piece of metal, plastic, etc. that is sewn onto a piece of clothing and used for fastening two parts together
words.example: (British English), to do up/undo your buttons

words.def: a place where people live temporarily in tents or temporary buildings
words.example: Let's return to camp.

words.def: living in a tent, etc. on holiday
words.example: Do you go camping?

words.def: a metal container in which food and drink is sold
words.example: can of something, a can of beans/beer/soda

words.def: having the form and size used at the beginning of a sentence or a name
words.example: English is written with a capital ‘E’.

words.def: the process of caring for somebody/something and providing what they need for their health or protection
words.example: Some people were badly injured and needed medical care.

words.def: giving attention or thought to what you are doing so that you avoid hurting yourself, damaging something or doing something wrong
words.example: Be careful!

words.def: with a lot of attention or thought, so that you avoid hurting yourself, damaging something or doing something wrong
words.example: Drive carefully.

words.def: a long pointed orange root vegetable
words.example: grated carrot

words.def: a humorous drawing in a newspaper or magazine, especially one about politics or events in the news
words.example: a political/satirical cartoon

words.def: a particular situation or a situation of a particular type
words.example: in… cases, In some cases people have had to wait several weeks for an appointment.

words.def: money in the form of coins or notes
words.example: How much cash do you have on you?

words.def: a large strong building with thick, high walls and towers, built in the past by kings or queens, or other important people, to defend themselves against attack
words.example: a medieval castle

words.def: to show that a day or an event is important by doing something special on it
words.example: Jake's passed his exams. We're going out to celebrate.

words.def: a coin and unit of money worth 1% of the main unit of money in many countries, for example of the US dollar or of the euro
words.example: A one-minute phone call to the UK cost 10 cents.

words.def: a period of 100 years
words.example: A century ago, Valparaiso was the country's main port.

words.def: strongly believing something; having no doubts
words.example: I think it was him, but I can't be certain.

words.def: without doubt
words.example: Without treatment, she will almost certainly die.

words.def: a possibility of something happening, especially something that you want
words.example: chance of doing something, She only has a slim chance of passing the exam.

words.def: a person or an animal in a book, play or film
words.example: the main character in the film

words.def: an act of making sure that something is safe, correct or in good condition by examining it
words.example: Could you give the tyres a check?

words.def: a person whose job is to cook, especially the most senior person in a restaurant, hotel, etc.
words.example: a new book by celebrity chef Jamie Oliver

words.def: an act of choosing between two or more possibilities; something that you can choose
words.example: We are faced with a difficult choice.

words.def: a building where Christians go to attend services, pray, etc.
words.example: The procession moved into the church.

words.def: a thin tube of paper filled with tobacco, for smoking
words.example: to smoke/have a cigarette

words.def: a completely round flat shape
words.example: the centre/circumference/radius/diameter of a circle

words.def: in a way that is easy to see or hear
words.example: Please speak clearly after the tone.

words.def: quick at learning and understanding things
words.example: a clever child

words.def: shut
words.example: Keep the door closed.

words.def: a grey or white mass that floats in the sky, made of very small drops of water
words.example: Dark clouds were gathering in the west.

words.def: the land next to or near to the sea or ocean
words.example: a trip to the coast

words.def: a system of words, letters, numbers or symbols that represent a message or record information secretly or in a shorter form
words.example: to break/crack a code (= to understand and read the message)

words.def: to bring things together from different people or places
words.example: collect something, to collect data/evidence/information

words.def: one of the straight sections from top to bottom into which text on a page or screen is divided
words.example: a dictionary with two columns per page

words.def: making you feel physically relaxed; pleasant to wear, sit on, etc.
words.example: It's such a comfortable bed.

words.def: happening often; existing in large numbers or in many places
words.example: a common problem/occurrence/practice

words.def: to share or exchange information, news, ideas, feelings, etc.
words.example: We only communicate by email.

words.def: a business organization that makes money by producing or selling goods or services
words.example: insurance/oil/tech/technology/pharmaceutical companies

words.def: to examine people or things to see how they are similar and how they are different
words.example: compare A and B, It is interesting to compare their situation and ours.

words.def: an event in which people compete with each other to find out who is the best at something
words.example: a design/an art/a talent competition

words.def: to say that you are annoyed, unhappy or not satisfied about somebody/something
words.example: She never complains, but she's obviously exhausted.

words.def: including all the parts, etc. that are necessary; whole
words.example: a complete list/sequence/picture/profile

words.def: the state that something is in
words.example: in… condition, to be in pristine/excellent/perfect condition

words.def: (of two or more things or people) having a link between them
words.example: We do not believe that the incidents are connected.

words.def: to think about something carefully, especially in order to make a decision
words.example: I'd like some time to consider.

words.def: the situation in which something happens and that helps you to understand it
words.example: Such databases are being used in a wide range of contexts.

words.def: one of the large land masses of the earth such as Europe, Asia or Africa
words.example: the continent of Africa

words.def: to keep existing or happening without stopping
words.example: If the current trend continues, that number will increase 165 per cent by 2050.

words.def: the power to make decisions about how a country, an area, an organization, etc. is run
words.example: control of somebody/something, The party expects to gain control of the council in the next election.

words.def: a person who cooks food or whose job is cooking
words.example: John is a very good cook (= he cooks well).

words.def: a large piece of equipment for cooking food, containing an oven and gas or electric rings on top
words.example: a gas cooker

words.def: the process of preparing food by heating it
words.example: My husband does all the cooking.

words.def: a thing that is made to be the same as something else, especially a document or a work of art
words.example: The thieves replaced the original painting with a copy.

words.def: a part of something where two or more sides, lines or edges join
words.example: the four corners of a square

words.def: in a way that is accurate or true, without any mistakes
words.example: Have you spelled it correctly?

words.def: the amount of money that you need in order to buy, make or do something
words.example: cost of something, the high/low cost of housing

words.def: an area of land that has or used to have its own government and laws
words.example: European countries

words.def: two people or things
words.example: I saw a couple of men get out.

words.def: not sensible; stupid
words.example: Are you crazy? We could get killed doing that.

words.def: to make something happen or exist
words.example: Scientists disagree about how the universe was created.

words.def: involving the use of skill and the imagination to produce something new or a work of art
words.example: a course on creative writing (= writing stories, plays and poems)

words.def: activities that involve breaking the law
words.example: This month's figures show an increase in violent crime.

words.def: a mark or an object formed by two lines crossing each other (X or +); the mark (X) is often used on paper to show something
words.example: I've put a cross on the map to show where the hotel is.

words.def: a large number of people gathered together in a public place, for example in the streets or at a sports game
words.example: He pushed his way through the crowd.

words.def: having a lot of people or too many people
words.example: We made our way through the crowded streets.

words.def: a piece of furniture with doors and shelves used for storing dishes, food, clothes, etc.
words.example: kitchen cupboards

words.def: a person or an organization that buys goods or services from a shop or business
words.example: marketing strategies to target potential customers

words.def: a person who dances or whose job is dancing
words.example: She's a fantastic dancer.

words.def: the possibility of something happening that will injure, harm or kill somebody, or damage or destroy something
words.example: Danger! Keep Out!

words.def: likely to injure or harm somebody, or to damage or destroy something
words.example: The situation is extremely dangerous.

words.def: with no or very little light, especially because it is night
words.example: a dark room/street/forest/night

words.def: no longer alive
words.example: My mother's dead; she died in 2017.

words.def: used at the beginning of a letter before the name or title of the person that you are writing to
words.example: Dear Sir or Madam

words.def: the fact of somebody dying or being killed
words.example: the anniversary of his wife’s death

words.def: to think carefully about the different possibilities that are available and choose one of them
words.example: It's up to you to decide.

words.def: a unit for measuring temperature
words.example: at… degrees, Water freezes at 32 degrees Fahrenheit (32°F) or zero/nought degrees Celsius (0°C).

words.def: a person whose job is to take care of people’s teeth
words.example: to see/visit/consult your dentist

words.def: according to
words.example: Starting salary varies from £26 000 to £30 500, depending on experience.

words.def: a piece of writing or speech that says what somebody/something is like; the act of writing or saying in words what somebody/something is like
words.example: Follow the link below for a more detailed description.

words.def: to damage something so badly that it no longer exists, works, etc.
words.example: The building was completely destroyed by fire.

words.def: to gradually grow or become bigger, more advanced, stronger, etc.; to make something do this
words.example: From birth to age 5, a child's brain develops rapidly.

words.def: a book in which you can write down the experiences you have each day, your private thoughts, etc.
words.example: Do you keep a diary (= write one regularly)?

words.def: to stop living
words.example: My father died suddenly at the age of 48.

words.def: the food and drink that you eat and drink regularly
words.example: to eat a healthy, balanced diet
